error_signature: RuntimeError: The NVIDIA driver on your system is too old (found version XXXXX)
error_traceback: RuntimeError: The NVIDIA driver on your system is too old (found version 12080). Please update your GPU driver by downloading and installing a new version from the URL: http://www.nvidia.com/Download/index.aspx Alternatively, go to: https://pytorch.org to install a PyTorch version that has been compiled with your version of the CUDA driver.
solution: Downgrade PyTorch to match the NVIDIA driver CUDA version. Steps: 1) Check driver version from error (e.g. 12080 = CUDA 12.0). 2) Find compatible PyTorch+CUDA combo from https://pytorch.org/get-started/previous-versions/. 3) Run: uv pip install torch==2.4.1+cu121 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121 (adjust version as needed). Common mappings: driver 12080 -> cu118 or cu121, driver 11080 -> cu118, driver 12040 -> cu121. Do NOT force CPU-only (device_map=cpu) as evaluation requires GPU for performance.
notes: This is an infrastructure issue. The correct fix is always to install a PyTorch version compiled for the available CUDA driver, never to skip GPU usage.
